message: That day, it was afternoon by the time I realized all the other people had disappeared from the world.
The first thing I noticed was that Kagami and Tsukasa were gone.
The house that had been so noisy before was now enveloped in silence. Not a single voice could be heard.
There was nothing but static on the TV no matter which channel I flipped to.
At first, I thought it was just a coincidence.
I thought Kagami and Tsukasa had gone home. I thought the TV antenna was just acting up.
I walked around the room with sleepy eyes.
I didn't start to get anxious until I got in the shower.
As the hot water pricked at my skin, my thoughts started to come together, and I began to notice that some things were off.
Since last night, I had hardly seen any other human beings.
I took some milk out of the fridge.
The stuff in the fridge hadn't gone bad. The power was still on.
I looked at the clock. It was still ticking away the seconds.
It ticked off each second, one by one, with a crisp sound.
I sat on the floor and looked around.
The room was really big.
I'd been living in this house alone up until now, but this was the first time I'd felt like I was living alone.
I chugged the rest of the milk and went back to my room.
I turned on the computer.
The servers were still up, so I could connect to the Internet.
My email inbox was empty. Nothing new.
My anxiety grew.
But then I felt a moment of relief.

name: Yuki
message: "There are new posts."
The news site I visit had been updated, and there were new posts on the anonymous text board.
Through the screen, I could feel the presence of people in the world.
The words were increasing. The words were expanding.
Time kept ticking away.
Second by second, unchanging.
But despite that...
Or perhaps... because of that?
The landscape had changed, like it was flickering. I felt a strange feeling every time the clock ticked away another second.
It felt like the world was contracting.

name: Yuki
message: "Now that I think about it, why haven't I seen Kagami and Tsukasa since last night?"
They were so insistent on staying here, but now...
They had left without a word.
In that case, why didn't I just go press the intercom and ask?
Their house was right next to mine. The two of them should've been over there.
The words on the Internet were still increasing.
Words were being written on every site.
That was my proof that people still existed.
This wasn't an imaginary world. It was the real world, where the news was still being updated minute by minute.
So why did I feel so uneasy?
The words continued expanding, but I had this feeling like the world was contracting.
As the world shrunk, the words expanded.
I turned the TV on again.
Static.
The signal was poor. Was the antenna mistuned?
Words still flowed through the telephone line.
No, not just words. Images and even videos were showing up.

name: Yuki
message: "What's this feeling? What the... Ahaha."
The television screen was still full of static. It wasn't receiving anything.

name: Yuki
message: "Hmph. I give up."
I brushed the hair out of my face as I let out a sardonic laugh and looked around the room.
I looked out the window.
A large sunflower was blooming.
For some reason, it looked gloomy.
They say sunflowers face toward the sun when they bloom.
They're flowers that symbolize brightness.
Regardless, the sunflower looked like a flower that had been put on someone's grave.
The sunflower's yellow color started to get on my nerves.
The static didn't stop.
The words increased. The computer screen was covered in words.
I took out my cellphone. I had zero bars of reception.

name: Yuki
message: "No signal at all?"
All the wired devices were still working.
But not a single radio signal was getting through.

name: Yuki
message: "Some sort of jamming, maybe?"
I tried the landline.
If I could use the Internet, I should've been able to use the phone.
I could hear the phone ringing next door.
Are Kagami and Tsukasa not home?
The atmosphere was unbearable. I got dressed and ran outside.
I stood in front of the Wakatsuki house.
Why didn't they answer the phone? Shouldn't someone be upset by all the weird stuff happening?

name: Yuki
message: "What's this?"
When I tried to ring the doorbell, I realized...
This house had no nameplate. There was just a white ceramic plate there.
I walked to the station.
The trains were running like normal.
But no one was there.
It wasn't a terribly large station, but it wasn't so small that the lack of people seemed normal, either.
A train came into the station.
There was no announcement.
I couldn't help but laugh.

name: Yuki
message: "If every trace of humanity was gone, then it wouldn't be that scary, but..."
Existences flickered before me. They were existences that I couldn't see or touch.

name: Yuki
message: "Ahaha... What is this? This is just like that ride from yesterday."
The Spirit Room.
Lifestyles that continued despite the people being cut out of them.
The gondola that kept moving forward and forward.
I rode the train.
I didn't see anyone even after coming all the way out here.
No one was at school. Even in Suginomiya, I didn't see anyone.
"Maybe you didn't see anyone because it's the weekend?"

name: Yuki
message: "Ahaha. Still, it's weird that I didn't see anyone at all."

name: Yuki
message: "Where are we?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Is there any meaning in that question?"

name: Yuki
message: "Who cares if there's meaning? Of course I'm going to ask! That's just natural!"

name: Zakuro
message: "Natural, is it?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Well then, I'll answer you."

name: Zakuro
message: "This is the roof of the tallest building of Kita High, Building C."

name: Zakuro
message: "The closest place to the sky."

name: Yuki
message: "No, that's not what I mean. This whole city."

name: Zakuro
message: "This ward is called Suginomiya."

name: Yuki
message: "That's not it. I didn't meet anyone on my way here, but there are still signs of human existence."

name: Zakuro
message: "The world is nothing but the world. If you ask what 'the world where I didn't meet anyone' is, there's nothing to say except that it is 'the world where you didn't meet anyone.'"

name: Yuki
message: "That's not what I mean!"

name: Zakuro
message: "I brought some tea. Would you like to have some?"

name: Yuki
message: "Tea?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."
The roof and the tea set.
Takashima-san and I.
The texture of the teacup Takashima-san handed me.
I smelled an aroma coming from the tea.
Earl Gray filled my nostrils.

name: Zakuro
message: "I picked out the best leaves. Please, relax."
The bitter taste and pleasant aroma grew within my mouth.
I glanced up at the sky.
The blue sky stretched as far as the eye could see.
The sunlight.
The sound of the wind.
Every sensation seemed to affirm the reality of this world.

name: Zakuro
message: "The internal world."
She let those words hang in the air.

name: Zakuro
message: "The internal world and the external world."

name: Zakuro
message: "Do you think they exist?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Is everything you're feeling right now part of the external world? Or part of the internal world?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Is this blue sky part of your internal world? Or is it part of the external world?"

name: Zakuro
message: "It is as if we each have our own internal world, and we only feel that we are all inhabiting a communal external world."

name: Zakuro
message: "So how does one reach the external world which we share?"

name: Yuki
message: "Reach..."

name: Zakuro
message: "By what means can a person truly know the world?"

name: Zakuro
message: "And by what means can a person come to false conclusions about the world?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Is there a way to determine whether a perception is correct?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Is there a limit to human knowledge? If so, in what form does that limit exist?"

name: Yuki
message: "What are you talking about all of a sudden?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Nothing. I just answered your question with more questions."

name: Zakuro
message: "Is this world right here your internal world? Or is it the external world?"

name: Yuki
message: "The internal world and... the external world?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. The internal world and the external world."

name: Zakuro
message: "It is from the external world that we get all information which can't be codified in language. It manifests in our consciousness through vision, the sense of touch, and all our other senses."

name: Zakuro
message: "External information, basic cognition, sense data. The nomenclature isn't important. We receive that information and use it to construct our inner worlds."

name: Zakuro
message: "If one believes that..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Then the world has a two-layered structure, with an internal and external world."

name: Zakuro
message: "In that case, how are those two worlds demarcated?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Is the flavor of the tea you just drank part of the external world? Is it part of the internal world?"

name: Zakuro
message: "How about the teacup you just touched? Is it part of the external world? Is it part of the internal world?"

name: Zakuro
message: "The person you are looking at right now... Am I part of your internal world? Am I part of the external world?"

name: Zakuro
message: "The object that existed before the information came from the external world..."

name: Zakuro
message: "What is the object that comes from the outside, and until what point is it external? From what point is it internal?"

name: Zakuro
message: "An object becomes part of the internal world at the moment that one senses it through sight, sound, or any other sense."

name: Zakuro
message: "So when can we reach this so-called external world?"

name: Zakuro
message: "So I answer your question thusly."

name: Zakuro
message: "Where are we?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Suginomiya Ward, Kita High, Building C, rooftop."

name: Zakuro
message: "I cannot give you a better answer than that."

name: Zakuro
message: "I have no way of viewing any world other than the one I am looking at now. I have no way of finding out about the external world."

name: Zakuro
message: "I can only gain information through the context of my own inner world. I can only speak to those questions which I am capable of knowing the answer to."
Takashima-san's expression was almost blank today.
It was like her expression had vanished along with the people of the city.

name: Yuki
message: "You sure know a lot of hard stuff, Takashima-san."

name: Zakuro
message: "No. They're all thoughts that you've had."

name: Zakuro
message: "I suppose you've realized by now."

name: Yuki
message: "The identity of the girl who is the world itself is..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."

name: Yuki
message: "Hey, Takashima-san."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."

name: Yuki
message: "Could I touch you?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, if that is what you desire."
I reached out to her.
The smell of her hair. The feel of her skin, her breath, her pulse. I could feel all of them.

name: Yuki
message: "You're warm."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."

name: Yuki
message: "Why did everyone disappear?"

name: Zakuro
message: "If there is no vessel or container for a liquid, then that liquid is unable to fill any space."

name: Zakuro
message: "You were the one who whispered that this earth is not a vessel."

name: Zakuro
message: "That is all."

name: Yuki
message: "A liquid without a vessel..."

name: Yuki
message: "That's me?"

name: Zakuro
message: "You could say that. Or you could say it's not."

name: Zakuro
message: "Putting that aside..."

name: Yuki
message: "What?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Shall we go see the beginning and end of the sky together?"

name: Yuki
message: "The beginning and end of the sky..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. The final sky."
The two of us looked up at the sky from the rooftop.
We laid down and looked at the great blue sky.
Her head came up to my arm, so she ended up using it as a pillow.

name: Zakuro
message: "It doesn't hurt, does it?"

name: Yuki
message: "Not at all."

name: Zakuro
message: "I see."

name: Yuki
message: "Takashima-san?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."

name: Yuki
message: "Does my arm make a good pillow?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. It's very comfortable."
Takashima-san laughed.
I definitely felt her. I could feel her touch, her fragrance, and her breath.
That's why I asked.

name: Yuki
message: "My smell, my pulse, my warmth..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Heheh. Don't worry. They're all right here."

name: Zakuro
message: "I'm not a figment of your imagination or an illusion, so long as you believe that to be so."
If I believe, then I exist.

name: Yuki
message: "Existence through belief... We're like gods."

name: Zakuro
message: "Heheh. Not at all."

name: Zakuro
message: "That's an incredibly natural prerequisite."

name: Zakuro
message: "Without belief in something, a person couldn't even muster the will to walk."

name: Yuki
message: "We aren't permitted to even walk through the maze of skepticism."

name: Zakuro
message: "Your feet will falter when you begin to doubt whether the next step will plunge you into the abyss."

name: Yuki
message: "Heh. In other words, don't doubt yourself."

name: Zakuro
message: "That's not what I'm saying. There are myriad answers without questions worth asking."

name: Zakuro
message: "I'm just saying that there's no meaning in asking them."

name: Zakuro
message: "After all..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Hah, haa..."
Her eyes suddenly started to droop.

name: Zakuro
message: "Regardless of whether you believe or not, I'm happy here next to you."

name: Zakuro
message: "It feels very, very nice."

name: Zakuro
message: "The warmth of the person I love is comfortable. That's all. Hmm..."
Takashima-san nuzzled into my arm, a tranquil expression on her face.
She said my arm was comfortable.
Was there any meaning in doubting her words?

name: Zakuro
message: "Oh, how I wish I could stay here forever."

name: Yuki
message: "Forever? My arm would go numb."

name: Zakuro
message: "Not that I want to lay here forever. Just..."

name: Zakuro
message: "I want to stay in our world."

name: Yuki
message: "Our world?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Right. This world is you, and this world is me."

name: Yuki
message: "What do you mean?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Who knows? What do I mean? Heheh."
The world was dyed red as the sun began to set.
Vega revealed her form in the eastern sky. Next, Deneb. Finally, Altair completed the shape of the Summer Triangle.
An enormous triangle stretched across the Milky Way.
Stars dashed across the sky.
The Summer Triangle approached the apex of the heavens.

name: Yuki
message: "The Summer Triangle is all the way up there."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."

name: Yuki
message: "We're almost out of time. Today is the last, isn't it?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."

name: Yuki
message: "The time is approaching when the girl of the sky will meet the girl who is the world itself, right?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."

name: Zakuro
message: "Well then..."
Takashima-san took the doll and held it over the fence.
I silently waited for the sky.

name: Zakuro
message: "Yuki-san."
I opened my eyes when I heard her say my name.
The doll she had thrown was floating in the air, and then it stood up.
I had thought that things were a little strange before, but this time I couldn't help but gasp at the sight before me.

name: Zakuro
message: "Excuse me, Mr. Stuffed Rabbit?"

name: Doll
message: "Yes?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Are you the station master?"

name: Doll
message: "No, not at all."

name: Zakuro
message: "Then who are you?"

name: Doll
message: "I'm the Rabbit Station Master."

name: Zakuro
message: "So you're Mr. Rabbit Station Master, then."

name: Doll
message: "Yes. I'm Mr. Rabbit Station Master. Um, putting that aside..."
The stuffed rabbit moved toward me.
He stopped in front of me and peered at me with eyes that looked like... No, eyes that really were buttons.

name: Yuki
message: "What?"

name: Doll
message: "Excuse me. Would you allow me to partake in one of your cigarettes?"

name: Yuki
message: "Sure..."
I wondered why he was here, but for the moment, I just passed him a cigarette.

name: Doll
message: "Oh... You have good taste. This is quite the rare brand."

name: Yuki
message: "Not really. It's sold at the convenience store, so it's not all that rare."

name: Doll
message: "Then that convenience store is what's rare."

name: Yuki
message: "Convenience stores are everywhere."

name: Doll
message: "Are they now? Also, could you give me a light?"
He was awfully demanding for a stuffed toy.

name: Yuki
message: "Sure."

name: Doll
message: "Whoa, whoa, be careful with that. I'm really flammable, you know?"

name: Yuki
message: "Then maybe you shouldn't be smoking?"

name: Doll
message: "That's a good point. Ahaha."
He laughed, though he didn't seem to pay any heed to my warning.
He lit the cigarette.

name: Doll
message: "Haaa... It tastes really good."

name: Yuki
message: "Well, yeah."

name: Doll
message: "You know, they say tobacco is bad for you, don't they?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah. It really is, you know?"

name: Doll
message: "Actually, that's a lie."

name: Yuki
message: "No. It's definitely not a lie."

name: Doll
message: "You don't get it. You don't get it at all. You can say that so easily because you don't smoke yourself."

name: Yuki
message: "No, I smoke. Why else would I have cigarettes on me?"

name: Doll
message: "I guess you're right. That's another good point. Ahaha."
He laughed me off. He really didn't care about my opinion at all.
The gray smoke disappeared into the darkness of the night.

name: Doll
message: "You know, they only recently discovered that tobacco isn't all that bad for you."

name: Doll
message: "They actually discovered it's good for you. They did research and proved it."

name: Yuki
message: "What? Who did research like that?"

name: Doll
message: "The Ministry of Health and Welfare."

name: Yuki
message: "No way."

name: Doll
message: "It's true. The newest statistical analysis bears it out. For young people, it's almost always good for the body."

name: Doll
message: "They say good medicine never tastes good, right? It's just like that."

name: Doll
message: "It means what's bitter is better for you."

name: Yuki
message: "That's wrong."

name: Doll
message: "It's not wrong."

name: Yuki
message: "Then what about a poison that tastes bitter?"

name: Doll
message: "Well, of course poison is going to be bitter."

name: Doll
message: "Poison and medicine are the same thing. Depending on how you take it and how much you use, poison can be medicine. That's why tobacco is a kind of medicine."

name: Doll
message: "Right now, the majority party is advocating the classification of tobacco as a designated health food."

name: Doll
message: "But still, this cigarette tastes really good."
What's up with this guy?
He flicked the cigarette away. It slowly fell to the ground and scattered cinders into the air upon impact.
His feet began to rise off the ground.
He took out his pocket watch.

name: Doll
message: "Please stay behind the white line. It's dangerous."

name: Yuki
message: "White line?"
At some point the fence had disappeared, and it was replaced by a white line.
It felt like I could step right off the roof and into thin air.

name: Yuki
message: "Huh... I guess anything can happen now."

name: Doll
message: "Not 'anything,' not anything at all. Come on, it's dangerous, so please step back."

name: Yuki
message: "What's that?"

name: Doll
message: "Oh, the next steamer is a special direct train to the Summer Triangle. Please step away from the white line."

name: Yuki
message: "Steamer?"

name: Yuki
message: "What the hell?"

name: Zakuro
message: "This is what surprises you, not the fact that the doll is the station master? Heheh."

name: Yuki
message: "This is a steam train?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. It's the Milky Way Railroad."

name: Doll
message: "The doors are opening."

name: Zakuro
message: "Well, let's go."

name: Yuki
message: "What do you mean, 'let's go'?"
Suddenly the rooftop was overflowing with people, like a train station.
Though I had no idea what they were.
"Aren't you going to get on?"
I suddenly heard a voice from behind me.
Even though no one was there a moment ago.
The man in front of me was covered in grime and wore tattered clothes.

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, well..."

name: Doll
message: "I'll inspect your tickets."
A teddy bear came out of the train and stood in front of me.

name: Yuki
message: "Who are you?"

name: Doll
message: "I'm the conductor, of course. Come on, if you're going to get on, please show me your ticket."
The conductor? He looked just like the stuffed animal that fell on me from the rooftop.

name: Zakuro
message: "Here."
Takashima-san took a small gray ticket from her pocket.

name: Doll
message: "And how about you?"

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, no, I don't..."
Even if I wanted to get on this mysterious train, it's not like I had a ticket.
I pulled out my pockets to show I didn't have anything and... pulled out a green piece of paper, about the size of a postcard, folded in four.
When he saw the paper, he took it from my hand.

name: Doll
message: "Let me see."
The conductor stood very straight and unfolded the paper carefully.
Then, as he read it, he kept fidgeting and straightening the buttons on his jacket.
What was that? Was it not a ticket after all?
The station master came over too, and they both peered up at the paper and studied it intensely.
I wondered if they were going to get mad at me...

name: Doll
message: "Hmm... You've got something really rare here. Did you bring this from three-dimensional space, sir?"
The conductor asked me that while handing the ticket back to me, but I had no idea how to respond.

name: Yuki
message: "Uh... I don't really know..."
I opened the paper, and it proved to be ten strange characters printed in the middle of a black arabesque pattern.

name: Yuki
message: "What's this..."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "My, this is very valuable..."

name: Yuki
message: "Is it?"

name: Mysterious Person
message: "I can't believe you could have something like this and not know about it... What you have here is a ticket that even lets you go to the real heaven."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "Not just heaven, wherever you like. Why, if you have this, you ought to be able to go anywhere at all on the likes of this Milky Way Railway of the imperfect, illusory fourth dimension."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "No, this really is quite something..."

name: Doll
message: "We only stop at this station for five minutes. Please board the train."

name: Yuki
message: "This station?"
The rooftop was... a train station.

name: Yuki
message: "What's the name of this station?"

name: Doll
message: "Suginomiya Kita High Rooftop Station."
Oh, just what you'd expect...
Takashima-san and I entered a carriage lit by a row of small yellow lights.
The seats in the carriage were covered with plush blue cloth and were almost all empty.
We sat in one of the many empty seats.
Then a man who we'd seen outside started talking to us.

name: Mysterious Person
message: "Well then, where would you like that ticket to take you?"

name: Yuki
message: "Ahaha... This is kind of embarrassing, but I actually don't know where we're going."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "I see... How about we say, 'I don't have any particular place to go, but I thought I'd ride the train to Osaka'?"

name: Yuki
message: "Does this train go to Osaka?"

name: Mysterious Person
message: "Of course it does."

name: Yuki
message: "But I don't really feel like going to Osaka..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Here, look at this map."

name: Yuki
message: "Map?"
Takashima-san took out a map that looked like a circular sheet.

name: Yuki
message: "That's a map..."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "Oh? You didn't receive one at the station?"
It was a sparkling stone that seemed to have been cut out of the night sky itself.

name: Yuki
message: "Is that obsidian?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, that's right. This map is made of obsidian."

name: Zakuro
message: "Look, if you hold it up to the window like this..."
At that moment everything before my eyes became suddenly bright as though the light from a trillion phosphorescent squid had been frozen at the same instance and dropped down into the sky.

Or as though a diamond company had pretended it was not making a good haul and had hoarded its diamonds in hopes of keeping the price high, but someone had suddenly turned the cache upside down and scattered diamonds everywhere.

name: Zakuro
message: "Is that moonlight shining on the river bed?"
We glanced out the window and watched as the silver pampas grass of the sky that grew among the palely shining banks of the Milky Way river rippled and swayed in the rustling wind.

name: Yuki
message: "That's not moonlight. It's on account of the silver river of the Milky Way that it shines so."
When I stuck my head out the window...
The Milky Way.

I became aware of beautiful water, more transparent than glass or even hydrogen, flowing soundlessly on. It might have been a trick of the eyes, but at times the water seemed to form tiny purple ripples or shine like a rainbow.
In the fields here and there stood beautiful phosphorescent triangular markers.
The far ones were small and sharply etched in orange and yellow. The near ones were large and had a slightly hazy pale blue light.
Set in various configurations, some triangular, some rectangular, some in the shape of a lightning bolt or loop of chain, they filled the fields with their light.

name: Mysterious Person
message: "It's beautiful, isn't it?"
The man whispered to himself, and something caught my eye.

name: Yuki
message: "Um, is that line on your hand a charm or something?"

name: Mysterious Person
message: "The line drawn on my hand? This is... something my master bestowed upon me."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "A very enthusiastic person, apt to draw such lines with a pen."
So his master drew a line on his hand with a pen. What a strange person.

name: Yuki
message: "Is there some meaning to that line?"

name: Mysterious Person
message: "Yes. Every one of these lines marks the parts of my body that my master is pleased with."
He touched one of the many lines drawn on his body and laughed mischievously.
That face looked like the same man's face and a completely different man's face at the same time.

name: Mysterious Person
message: "It may be unforgivable that I lived to be eighty-two. My satisfaction on that account was, however, less than you might think."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "You are right that a great longing for survival possessed me continually. I was in continual fear of death and continually struggling with it."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "I believe that the struggle against death, the unconditional and self-willed determination to live, is the motive power behind the lives and activities of all outstanding men."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "My eighty-two years showed just as conclusively that we must all die in the end as if I had died a schoolboy."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "If it helps to justify me I should like to say this too; my nature had much of the child in it, its curiosity and love for idleness and play."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "Well, and so it went on and on, till I saw that sooner or later there must be enough of play."
When he read out that description, I got the feeling that I knew him from somewhere.

name: Zakuro
message: "Where have you come from?"
Takashima-san asked that of the man in front of us.

name: Mysterious Person
message: "I was in the service of a wise master, but for a time I separated from my master and approached the house of my kin."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "That was because I thought more people than just myself should know of my existence."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "I had many friends in the mansion, and many guests came every day."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "People who were shocked, who laughed, who cried, who were enraged. I met with many people like that."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "That was the Wonderful Everyday that my master allowed me."

name: Zakuro
message: "Your master truly valued you, didn't he?"

name: Mysterious Person
message: "Yes, truly. Even after I've aged many years and been burned by the flame, I still have the ability to come here."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "I suppose this is what they call destiny."

name: Zakuro
message: "Would you mind if I touched your hand?"

name: Mysterious Person
message: "Of course, if you wish."
Takashima-san softly grasped the man's outstretched hand.

name: Zakuro
message: "Seriousness is an accident of time. It consists, I don't mind telling you in confidence, of putting too high a value on time."

name: Zakuro
message: "I, too, once put too high a value on time. For that reason I wished to be a hundred years old. In eternity, however, there is no time, you see. Eternity is a mere moment, just long enough for a joke."

name: Mysterious Person
message: "Are you able to read the line?"

name: Zakuro
message: "I can read it because it is. Because your master drew it on you."

name: Zakuro
message: "As a playwright shapes a drama from a handful of characters, so do we from the pieces of the disintegrated self build up ever new groups, with ever new interplay and suspense, and new situations that are eternally inexhaustible."

name: Zakuro
message: "It takes hold of some music played where you please, without distinction, stupid and coarse, lamentably distorted, to boot, and chucks it into space to land where it has no business to be."

name: Zakuro
message: "And yet after all this it cannot destroy the original spirit of the music; it can only demonstrate its own senseless mechanism, its inane meddling and marring."

name: Zakuro
message: "Listen, then, you poor thing. Listen well. You have need of it."

name: Zakuro
message: "And now you hear not only a Handel who, disfigured by radio, is, all the same, in this most ghastly of disguises still divine."

name: Zakuro
message: "You hear as well and you observe, most worthy sir, a most admirable symbol of all life. When you listen to radio you are a witness of the everlasting war between idea and appearance, between time and eternity, between the human and the divine."

name: Zakuro
message: "It little becomes people like you to be critics of radio or of life either. Better learn to listen first! Learn what is to be taken seriously and laugh at the rest."

name: Yuki
message: "Is that... how it is?"
The man looked at my expression and understood everything.

name: Yuki
message: "I remember it being smaller than that, though. It was pocket sized."

name: Book
message: "That's right... You often took me along to the cafe."

name: Book
message: "Please look at the cross in the stars. Does it make you remember a poem from another day?"
When I looked out the window...

Far downstream, emerging like a single tree out of the invisible water of the river, a cross studded with lights of blue, bitter-orange and every color under the sun and crowned with a pale white halo of cloud.
"But we above you ever more residing, in the ether's star translumined ice
Know not day nor night nor time's dividing
Wear not age nor sex for our device
All your sins and anguish self-affrighting
Your murders and lascivious delighting
Are to us as but a show, like the suns that circling go,
Changing not our day for night;
On your frenzied life we spy, and refresh ourselves thereafter,
With the stars in order fleeing;
Our breath is winter; in our sight
Fawns the dragon of the sky;
Cool and unchanging is our eternal being;
Cool and star bright is our eternal laughter."
A current of excitement ran through the train.
All that could be heard here and there was a happy cry, as when a child leaps up and plucks a gourd, or a sigh, ineffably deep and humble.
Then, gradually, the cross came abreast of their window and they could see the circlet of clouds, pale as the flesh of apples, slowly, gently turning.

"Hallelujah! Hallelujah!" the voices of the people resounded bright and joyful. And from the farthest reaches of the sky, that chill sky, everyone heard the indescribably fresh, transparent call of a trumpet.
Then, passing a cluster of signals and electric lamps, the train gradually slowed, until at last it came to a full stop directly opposite the cross.

name: Book
message: "I'm happy that I had the chance to meet you."

name: Book
message: "We'll walk from space to space in glad progression."

name: Book
message: "And should not cling to one as homestead for us."

name: Book
message: "The cosmic spirit will not bind nor bore us."

name: Book
message: "It lifts and widens us in ev'ry session."

name: Book
message: "For hardly set in one of life's expanses, we make it home, and apathy commences."

name: Book
message: "But only he, who travels and takes chances, can break the habits' paralyzing stances."

name: Book
message: "It might be, even, that the last of hours will make us once again a youthful lover."

name: Book
message: "The call of life to us forever flowers..."

name: Book
message: "Anon, my heart: Say farewell and recover!"

name: Yuki
message: "Thank you."
In the next instant, his form had disappeared.
The train was over half-empty now. All at once, it had become lonely and deserted with the wind rushing in.
We looked and saw all the people kneeling devoutly on the shores of the Milky Way river in front of the cross.
We saw a god-like figure robed in white approaching with outstretched arms across the invisible water of the heavenly river.
But at that moment a glassy whistle blew and, just as the train was beginning to move, a silvery mist rolled smoothly in from farther downstream and blocked their view.

name: Zakuro
message: "Yuki-san."
Then, the only passengers left were the two of us.

name: Zakuro
message: "There's the Coal Sack over there. It's a hole in the sky."
I looked where she was pointing.

In one part of the heavenly river gaped a huge pitch-black hole. I could not tell how deep the bottom was or what might be inside. No matter how much I peered into it, rubbing my eyes, I could make nothing out; only my eyes began to smart.
Those were all lines out of Night on the Milky Way Railroad.
Indeed, our story ended with a story.

name: Yuki
message: "This is where Giovanni and Campanella have to part ways."

name: Yuki
message: "This was the final stop for their story."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, that's right."

name: Yuki
message: "Then, from here on out, I have to walk alone."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."

name: Yuki
message: "I see. So that's how it is."
When had I realized?
No, I got the feeling there was no "when." I'd probably understood everything from that moment.

name: Yuki
message: "The girl who is the world itself was me."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."

name: Yuki
message: "And the beginning and end of the sky that you were looking for..."

name: Yuki
message: "That was the end of this illusionary world's sky and the beginning of the sky in the real world."

name: Yuki
message: "So you were looking for the border between the illusionary world and the real world."

name: Zakuro
message: "The illusionary world and the real world, is it? I wish they were so clearly demarcated."

name: Zakuro
message: "I just tried to return you to your original world."

name: Zakuro
message: "Because you got caught up in this."

name: Yuki
message: "I got caught up, huh?"
From that twilit rooftop.
A girl jumped over the fence.
I saw it happen.

name: Yuki
message: "I definitely did hear a loud sound, at least for a moment."

name: Yuki
message: "The moment that doll touched my skin..."

name: Yuki
message: "That evening, it wasn't a doll that fell from the rooftop, was it?"

name: Zakuro
message: "No. That was me."

name: Yuki
message: "You were the one who fell."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes."

name: Yuki
message: "And that's how you hit me?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes. That's how it happened."

name: Yuki
message: "I see."

name: Yuki
message: "Which means that the destination of this Milky Way Railroad is a pretty boring place."

name: Zakuro
message: "A boring place?"

name: Yuki
message: "After all, wouldn't that mean we're going to wake up in a hospital bed?"

name: Zakuro
message: "I don't understand."

name: Yuki
message: "You don't?"

name: Zakuro
message: "No, not really."

name: Yuki
message: "Or we might even wake up in Heaven."

name: Zakuro
message: "No, I don't think that world exists."

name: Yuki
message: "Then Hell, maybe?"

name: Zakuro
message: "That world doesn't exist, either."

name: Zakuro
message: "The world ahead is a familiar sight. It is a world of filled with ordinary sights."

name: Yuki
message: "So at the very least, I'll be going back somewhere."

name: Zakuro
message: "Yes, beneath the sky where the three stars of Vega, Altair, and Deneb shine."

name: Yuki
message: "Beneath the sky..."

name: Zakuro
message: "This is our parting."

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, I guess it is."

name: Zakuro
message: "I'm sorry."

name: Yuki
message: "You don't need to apologize anymore."

name: Zakuro
message: "Then please let me say one more thing."

name: Yuki
message: "One more thing?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Thank you."

name: Zakuro
message: "You were the one who gave me life."

name: Zakuro
message: "I always loved you."

name: Zakuro
message: "I'm sure I was just a nuisance, but I'm thankful that I was able to meet you again in this world."

name: Zakuro
message: "I'm thankful that you let me experience something so wonderful, even as I was walking this road that I must walk alone."

name: Zakuro
message: "In my short life here in this world that you call an illusion, I made many memories."

name: Zakuro
message: "Even if it is an illusionary world, they're the best memories I have."

name: Zakuro
message: "Even if it is just a cheap, good-for-nothing dream world. Even then, the time I spent with you..."
"Every day was wonderful."
The world was covered in white.
Unbroken white.
A world of nothing but white.
An astonishing world.
A world where you could neither move forward nor run away.
But still, I forged ahead.
Even though the scenery around me didn't change at all, I kept on running.
Takashima-san...
Obstinance, vanity, scruples, discrimination, pride.
Takashima-san left all those bothersome things behind.
But I...
I would continue to demand my tobacco, my rooftop, and my frilly dress.
If that was the road by which I was supposed to return.
I remembered the sunflowers.
And I realized something.
Sunflowers bring to mind an offering of flowers.
Actually, he was the one who said that.
They look like chrysanthemums because they both belong to Astaraceae.
I had walked through this white world before.
This was the second time I had to walk this road.
This inescapable white world...
It was just like the road beyond that field of sunflowers.

name: Yuki
message: "I exist if I desire it, huh?"
In that white world, I remembered it all.
I knew that when I woke from this dream, I would have forgotten most of it.
But...
I pointed to the end of that endless white pathway.

name: Yuki
message: "The world is a vessel."

name: Yuki
message: "That which can fill the vessel..."
I pressed to my chest the truth I'd found and exited the white room.
I was done being a ghost.
Yuki opened her eyes.
